tone_of_voice: Tone: Clear, confident, and friendly. Conversational but professional — never stiff, never overly casual. Use "you" throughout. Active voice. Key topics: Speed and simplicity of document workflows, affordability for small businesses and self-employed professionals, no technical setup required. What resonates: Practical, outcome-led messaging. Lead with what the user can do and achieve: "Sign documents in seconds," "Work from anywhere," "No printing, no scanning." Clarity beats cleverness. Social proof: Strong — use "Trusted by 68M+ users" and "1M+ ready-to-use templates" as credibility anchors. Emphasize: No IT setup, no hidden fees, works on any device, 1M+ templates ready to use, legally binding e-signatures. Avoid: Jargon, enterprise-speak, filler phrases ("robust solution," "leverage," "seamlessly"). Avoid vague promises without a concrete payoff. Don't over-explain — trust the reader.

Tone: Restrained, elegant, logical. Always "vous". No exclamation marks. Quality of writing matters — poor grammar destroys credibility. Key topics: Efficiency, clarity, RGPD compliance. French audiences want rational arguments, not emotional appeals. What resonates: Clear logic — here's the problem, here's how we solve it, here's the proof. Value proposition must be explicit and structured. Social proof: Works when framed rationally, not emotionally. Emphasize: RGPD-conforme, ready to use without IT ("Prêt à l'emploi, sans DSI"), time saved, document completeness. Avoid: American-style hype, vague superlatives ("la meilleure solution"), multiple exclamation marks.

Tone: Warm, friendly, approachable. Use "tú" form. Not too corporate. Key topics: Reducing paperwork and admin burden ("el papeleo"), saving time, affordability for small business owners and freelancers (autónomos). What resonates: Anything that simplifies document workflow is seen as real value. Lead with practical outcomes: "sign documents in seconds", "send without printing." Social proof: Strong — use "68M+ users trust pdfFiller" prominently. Emphasize: No hidden costs, no IT setup needed, ready-to-use templates = time saved. Avoid: Cold corporate tone, vague promises, marketing hyperbole.

Tone: Energetic, warm, conversational. Use "você". Can use light storytelling. Key topics: Brazilian bureaucracy is a huge daily pain — anything reducing it lands well. Price accessibility for MEI and small businesses is critical. What resonates: Emotional framing works — "Imagine signing a document in seconds, without printing anything." Mobility matters — many users work from mobile. Social proof: Very effective — "Milhões já confiam" (millions already trust) is a strong hook. Emphasize: No bureaucracy, no printing, affordable pricing, works on any device, no IT needed. Avoid: Overly formal tone, cold corporate language.

Tone: Direct, no-nonsense, pragmatic. Use "jij/je" for SMB/freelancers, "u" for more formal B2B. Short sentences. Key topics: Concrete value, AVG (GDPR) compliance, price transparency, no hidden costs. What resonates: "What exactly do I get?" — answer it immediately and directly. ZZP'ers (freelancers) are a huge segment: emphasize solo-use simplicity. Social proof: Works when stated plainly as a fact, not as emotional proof. Emphasize: AVG-conform, geen verborgen kosten (no hidden costs), geen IT-afdeling nodig (no IT department needed), klaar in minuten (ready in minutes). Avoid: Marketing fluff, vague promises, overly warm or emotional tone.

Tone: Precise, neutral, structured. Use "Sie" in B2B contexts. No exclamation marks in headlines. Key topics: Data security (DSGVO/GDPR compliance is mandatory to mention), reliability, efficiency. What resonates: Specific facts and numbers, not emotions. "68M users" and "1M+ templates" work as hard proof. Legal validity of e-signatures matters. Social proof: Works when backed by concrete data — avoid vague superlatives. Emphasize: DSGVO-konform, sofort einsatzbereit (ready to use), reduces time spent on documents. Avoid: Hype language ("amazing", "revolutionary"), empty claims without evidence.

Tone: Human, warm, aesthetically aware. Use "Lei" or "voi" for B2B. Not robotic. Key topics: Simplicity for small and family businesses (PMI), compliance with Italian digital signature standards (eIDAS), reducing admin burden. What resonates: Design and visual quality signal trustworthiness. Concrete benefits over abstract features. "Easy to use, no IT department required." Social proof: Effective — "68 milioni di utenti" lands well. Emphasize: eIDAS/EU compliance, no IT setup, affordable for PMI, 1M+ ready templates. Avoid: Overly technical or robotic tone, Americanized hype.
